description_long: This is a stone water well set on a patch of dense, dark green grass with a slightly irregular, earthy base. The well itself is constructed from stacked, rectangular gray stones forming a cylindrical shaft, and it is topped with a pitched, terracotta-tiled roof supported by two wooden posts. The roof has a simple, rustic design with visible wooden beams and a slight overhang. There is no visible pulley, bucket, or water-drawing mechanism attached to the well. The surrounding grassy area is thick and unkempt, suggesting a natural or minimally maintained environment. No decorative elements or protective barriers are present around the well.
description: A rustic stone water well with a terracotta-tiled roof, set on a patch of dense grass and earth, with no visible water-drawing mechanism or decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone well, wooden posts, and the full terracotta-tiled roof, with thick grass on both sides.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the triangular profile of the roof and the stone shaft, with grass extending around the well.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the well and roof, with a clear path in the grass leading up to the well.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the roof's triangular end and the stone well, surrounded by grass and a visible dirt path.
attribute_well_cover_design: Pitched roof made of terracotta tiles supported by wooden posts; no lid or grating over the well opening.
